## Files matching `**/*.ts`

## Skill trigger: `ddlapi-using`

When writing TypeScript that imports `@netcracker/qubership-apihub-ddlapi` —
calling `buildFromDdl`, traversing a `Realm`/`Schema`/`Table`, or constructing
schema objects with its factories — apply the `ddlapi-using` skill.

## Skill trigger: `json-crawl-using`

When writing TypeScript that imports `@netcracker/qubership-apihub-json-crawl` —
calling `crawl`/`syncCrawl`, wiring hooks or path rules, or using `clone`/
`syncClone`/`transform` — apply the `json-crawl-using` skill.

## Files matching `**/*.{ts,tsx}`

## Skill trigger: `api-doc-viewer-using`

When integrating `@netcracker/qubership-apihub-api-doc-viewer` components into
application or portal UI — embedding viewers, wiring merged documents, or
configuring diff display — apply the `api-doc-viewer-using` skill.

## Files matching `**/{apm.yml,SKILL.md,*.instructions.md,*.prompt.md,*.agent.md,CLAUDE.md,AGENTS.md}`

## Skill trigger: `apm-authoring`

When editing any APM primitive (`apm.yml`, `*.instructions.md`, `SKILL.md`, `*.prompt.md`, `*.agent.md`, hooks, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`), apply the `apm-authoring` skill.

In a repo that uses APM, do not edit `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, or deployed skill files directly — they are regenerated by `apm install` / `apm compile`. Edit the matching primitive under `.apm/` and re-run `apm compile`.
